raw.title: Masonry — Bearing & Uplift at Steel Beam Seat
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Masonry — Bearing & Uplift at Steel Beam Seat 🖨 Print / PDF Masonry — Bearing & Uplift at Steel Beam Seat Effective bearing area (MDG REK-07) + Headed anchor uplift capacity (MDG REK-09) — ASD TMS 402-22 ASD §4.4.4 Effective Bearing Area §8.1.6 Bearing Stress §8.1.4.3.1 Anchor Tension MDG 2022 REK-07 & REK-09 A2 A1 lp = 10.00" e = 0.75" bp = 5.00" t = 7.63" Plan — Effective Bearing Area (Layout C) CMU Wall (Open Cells) Bond-Beam Course Grouted Cell(s) at Anchors Steel Beam (W16 profile) lbe = 5.25" lp = 10.00" Rdown = 17.4 k Rup = 5.2 k Elevation — Bearing & Uplift 1 — Wall & Material Properties Masonry Compressive Strength, f'm (psi) Specify per TMS 602 Nominal Wall Thickness (in) 4 in. nominal (3.63 in. actual) 6 in. nominal (5.63 in. actual) 8 in. nominal (7.63 in. actual) 10 in. nominal (9.63 in. actual) 12 in. nominal (11.63 in. actual) 16 in. nominal (15.63 in. actual) Actual thickness: 7.63 in. Bond Type Running Bond Stack Bond (Other than Running Bond) 2 — Bearing Plate & Layout (REK-07) SELECT LAYOUT Layout A Centered plate Running bond REK-07a Layout B Edge/eccentric plate Running bond REK-07b Layout C Centered plate Stack bond REK-07c Plate Width, bp (in) — through-wall direction Must be ≤ wall actual thickness Plate Length, lp (in) — along wall Distance to Nearest Head Joint, dj (in) Stack bond only: distance from plate edge to nearest head joint 3 — Applied Loads (Governing ASD Reactions) Enter the governing unfactored bearing reaction and uplift reaction from your load combination analysis (ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1). Downward governs for bearing (typically D+S or D+0.75S+0.75L). Uplift governs from 0.6D − W or 0.6D − 0.7E. Governing Downward Reaction, Rdown (lb) Governing Uplift Reaction, Rup (lb, enter as positive) 4 — Uplift Anchors (REK-09) TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 Anchor Type Headed Studs Headed Bolts Number of Anchors, n Anchor Diameter, da (in) Nominal shank diameter Anchor Tensile Strength, fu (psi) 60,000 psi typical for A307/headed studs; 58,000 psi for A36 Effective Embedment, lbe (in) From anchor head to bearing surface of plate; must be > t/2 Plate Length in Stud Direction, lp,stud (in) Plate dimension parallel to anchor row (used in Apt calculation) ▶ Run Checks ✓ ALL CHECKS PASS Maximum D/C = 0.920 | Bearing: 0.492 | Anchor (breakout): 0.920 | Anchor (steel): 0.197 BEARING GEOMETRY Property Value Notes Wall actual thickness, t 7.63 in. 7.63 in. (nominal 8 in.) Bearing plate, bp × lp 5.00 × 10.00 in. Layout C — Centered, Stack Bond Actual bearing area, A1 50.00 in.² bp × lp Effective spread distance, e 0.75 in. Limited by head joint distance Expanded area, A2 74.75 in.² At base of 45° pyramid √(A2/A1) 1.223 Effective bearing area, Abr 61.14 in.² min(A1√(A2/A1), 2A1) = min(61.14, 100.00) 17.38 k Bearing Load Rdown 5.22 k Uplift Load Rup 284.26 psi Bearing Stress fbr 577.50 psi Allowable Fb = 0.33f'm 108.42 in.² Projected Anchor Area Apt 5.67 k Anchor Capacity Ballow min(Bab, Bas) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Bearing Stress fbr Abr = 61.14 in.² (Layout C — Centered, Stack Bond) TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 fbr = 284.26 psi Fb = 577.50 psi 0.492 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.6 — Allowable Bearing Stress Allowable bearing stress: F b = 0.33f' m = 0.33 × 1750 psi = 577.50 psi TMS 402-22 §4.4.4 — Effective Bearing Area (Layout C — Centered, Stack Bond) A 1 = b p × l p = 5.00 × 10.00 = 50.00 in.² e nom = t − bp 2 = 1.31 in.; d j = 0.75 in. Spread = min(e nom , d j ) = min(1.31, 0.75) = 0.75 in. A 2 = (b p +2e)(l p +2e) = 6.50 × 11.50 = 74.75 in.² TMS 402 Eq. 4-1: A 1 √ A2/A1 = 50.00 × 1.223 = 61.14 in.² TMS 402 Eq. 4-2: 2A 1 = 2 × 50.00 = 100.00 in.² Abr = min(61.14, 100.00) = 61.14 in.² f br = Rdown Abr = 17378.00 lb 61.14 in.² = 284.26 psi D/C = 284.26 psi 577.50 psi = 0.492 Anchor Tension — Masonry Breakout Apt = 108.42 in.², 2 anchors, lbe = 5.25 in. TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 Eq. 8-1 Rup = 5.22 k Bab = 5.67 k 0.920 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 Eq. 8-1 — Masonry Breakout (Anchor Group) Projected tension area, Apt (anchor group, per MDG REK-09): Each anchor projects a breakout circle of radius l be onto the wall face. The full circle areas (πl be ² each) are reduced by the overlap of adjacent circles (s = 7.00 in. < 2l be = 10.50 in.) and by the portions falling outside the wall faces. Conservative approximation: inscribed rectangles of full wall thickness t. Half-chord at wall face, c = √ lbe² − (t/2)² = √ 5.25² − 3.81² = 3.61 in. beyond each end anchor Circle reach at mid-spacing = √ lbe² − (s/2)² = 3.91 in. > t/2 = 3.81 in. → full thickness engaged between anchors A pt = t × [(n−1)s + 2c] = 7.63 × [(2−1)×7.00 + 2×3.61] = 7.63 × 14.22 = 108.42 in.² Eq. 8-1: B ab = 1.25 A pt √ f'm = 1.25 × 108.42 × √ 1750 psi = 1.25 × 108.42 × 41.83 = 5669.28 lb D/C = 5216.00 lb 5669.28 lb = 0.920 Anchor Tension — Steel Yielding n=2, da=0.75 in., fu=60,000 psi TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 Eq. 8-2 Rup = 5.22 k Bas = 26.51 k 0.197 PASS ▶ Calc TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 Eq. 8-2 — Steel Yielding of Anchors A b = πda² 4 = π×0.75² 4 = 0.4418 in.² per anchor Eq. 8-2: B as = 0.5 n A b f u = 0.5 × 2 × 0.4418 in.² × 60,000 psi = 26507.19 lb D/C = 5216.00 lb 26507.19 lb = 0.197 Governing uplift capacity: Masonry Breakout (Eq. 8-1) governs — B allow = min(5.67, 26.51) = 5.67 k Engineering Notes: Bearing stress check per TMS 402-22 §8.1.6: Fb = 0.33f'm. This limit applies to the effective bearing area Abr, not the plate area A1. Anchor tension capacity Bab (Eq. 8-1) is computed for the anchor group using total projected area Apt = t×(lp,stud+2lbe). Verify that individual anchor cone areas do not overlap or extend beyond edges of masonry beyond what is modeled. For anchors near edges of the wall or near adjacent anchor groups, the projected area Apt must be reduced per TMS 402 §8.1.4.3.1. This calculator assumes full unreduced Apt. Load combinations should be per ASCE 7-22 §2.4.1. The downward and uplift reactions entered above should reflect the governing ASD combinations from a separate load calculation (see MDG REK-09 for approach). Stiffeners in the steel beam web may be required per AISC 360-22 §J10.2/J10.3. Web local yielding and crippling are not checked here. SJI Standard Specifications require the edge of the bearing plate be ≤ ½ in. from the face of masonry. Centering the plate (Layout A) requires the joist seat to be designed for the additional eccentricity. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub×

audit.dimensions: PASS: bp=5, lp=10, t=7.63, lbe=5.25 and loads match fixture/math
missing_pre_fix: MDG stud/embed geometry and adjacent grouted cells
audit.note: post-fix re-judgment 2026-07-11 (Claude reviewer): all required elements present in rebuilt diagram; screenshots in tools/masonry-audit/diagram-review/. Pre-fix failure retained in git history (commit 9c3596f).
